title: Separate required object ID from optional patch fields
description:
Update input structs mix a required id (the object to update) with optional patch fields. Because id has no default, Default cannot be derived, so callers must write out every field even when patching just one:

ChatUpdateInput { id: 'abc', muted: Some(true), mute_until: None, receive_typing_indicators: None, pinned_message_ids: None, ... } // 13 fields

Fix: move id to a direct method parameter, rename struct to *Patch, derive Default:

client.chat_update(&session, 'abc', &ChatPatch { muted: Some(true), ..Default::default() })

Affected update structs (5):
- ChatUpdateInput -> ChatPatch
- MessageUpdateInput -> MessagePatch (also: reaction_changes: &[] becomes absent by default)
- PresenceStatusSetInput -> PresenceStatusPatch
- ChatContactSetInput -> ChatContactPatch
- SpaceUpdateInput -> SpacePatch

Method signatures change: the id parameter moves out of the struct and becomes the second argument after session.
